title: Redesign auth system with OAuth2 PKCE and multi-tenant RBAC
description: Replace the current JWT/session-based authentication with OAuth2 PKCE flow, implement refresh token rotation with secure storage, add multi-tenant authentication with organization-scoped permissions, extend RBAC across all API routes, add per-tenant rate limiting, and migrate user schema to support multiple identity providers. Affects auth/, middleware/, API routes, and database schema.
existing_patterns: src/auth/jwt-service.ts - JWT signing/verification with blacklist, HS256 algorithm, access/refresh token generation
src/auth/session-store.ts - Session management with token indexing, max sessions per user, generation tracking for token rotation
src/middleware/rate-limiter.ts - SlidingWindowRateLimiter class with per-key tracking
src/middleware/role-guard.ts - requireRole() guard function for RBAC checks
src/auth/withAuth.ts - withAuth HOC wrapping API routes with role-based access control
src/collections/Users.ts - User schema with role field (admin/editor/viewer), organization field, refreshToken storage
src/auth/auth-service.ts - AuthService with login/refresh/verifyAccessToken/logout methods using Payload CMS
questions: What OAuth2 providers (Google, GitHub, etc.) should be supported for the identity provider migration?
Should existing JWT-based sessions be migrated to OAuth2 tokens or invalidated on deployment?
What is the expected tenant isolation model - separate databases per tenant or shared database with tenant_id filtering?
